rulings.Case Title: XI LUAN et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (CCSF) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Permitting Reproduction Of Photographs Of Decendent
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF XI LUAN, YUE LUAN, YING LUAN, ESTATE OF MARIE GUO Motion For An Order Permitting Reproduction Of Photographs Of Decendent. Denied. Plaintiffs have failed to show service of the request for court order on the district attorney as required by Code of Civil Procedure 129. Moreover, there is no good cause to order reproduction of the photographs or production of decedent's clothing since defendant does not contest that he hit the decedent and caused her death. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Julian Signes' Answers At Deposition And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Docs; Memorandum Of P&A; Declaration Of David Grassi; Separate Statement; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DAVID GRASSI's Motion To Compel Defendant Julian Signes' Answers At Deposition And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Defendant Signes has already been deposed by Plaintiff for six hours in the presence of a court-appointed discovery referee in this limited civil case in which Plaintiff's complaint seeks seven hundred fifty dollars ($750.00). The evidence shows that the documents responsive to the request for production accompanying Plaintiff's notice of Defendant's deposition have been produced. Plaintiff's motion is brought without substantial justification. Sanctions in the amount of $3,000.00 are awarded against Plaintiff. See Do v. Superior Court (2003) 109 Cal. App. 4th 1210, 1218 (holding that discovery sanctions may be awarded in favor of the prevailing party in a discovery dispute even where the prevailing party is represented by counsel on a pro-bono basis). Plaintiff shall pay defendant Julien Signes the sum of $3,000.00 within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT VERNON LOUCKS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied in its entirety. There are triable issues regarding if and when defendants converted plaintiff's stock. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this action because it is complaining about harm that is particular to it, not merely corporate harm. There are triable issues whether Vernon Loucks was a majority shareholder and whether Charles Loucks was the managing member. There are triable issues whether defendants breached their tort and contractual duties to plaintiff. Lillienthal does not apply to this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT VERNON LOUCKS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. The motion is procedurally improper. The third cause of action seeks damages and defendants do not address that issue. Nor do defendants state a proper claim for declaratory relief. The declaration that defendants seek is not necessary or proper because the release can be raised as a defense. (Code of Civil Procedure 1061). Moreover, defendants are essentially bringing a disguised motion to strike or motion in limine. The motion violates Code of Civil Procedure 437c(f)(1) because defendants are not seeking to dispose completely of any of plaintiff's causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGUS BEAN et al VS. THE TRAVELERS INSURANCE COMPANIES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defts Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ANGUS BEAN, GENE MAZE, SATYRICON INC., MARIE STRANE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Plaintiff's motion to compel production of claims adjuster Nelson Rayos's 10/13/2007 @ 13:14:46 note previously withheld under a claim of attorney-client privilege is denied as moot given that Defendant has produced the unredacted note in connection with Defendant's opposition to Plaintiff's motion. Plaintiff's motion to compel is also denied with respect to claims adjuster Nelson Rayos's 09/21/2007 @ 13:51:18 note. Mr. Rayos's declaration in opposition to the motion to compel states that the note describes a conversation he had with Traveler's insured, the sole purpose of which was to gather information so that the insurance company could defend its insured against a possible cross-complaint. Mr. Rayos's declaration also states that if Travelers' insureds had been sued, he would have retained counsel to provide that defense and would have provided to that defense counsel the information he gathered and entered in that claim note. Under the holding of Soltani-Rastegar v. Superior Court (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 424, this information, gathered by a claims adjuster, "for the sole purpose of defending" against claims, is protected by the attorney-client privilege. The fact that litigation was only a threat on the horizon and an attorney had not yet been selected to try to avert or meet that threat does not change the result. Id. The court declines plaintiff's invitation to order an in-camera review of the unredacted document in order to test the claim of privilege. Evidence Code Section 915 prohibits the court from requiring an in-camera review in order to rule on a claim that the document is protected by the attorney-client privilege. Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Superior Court (2009) 47 Cal.4th 725, 736. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WEILI DAI VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 1, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. The court has jurisdiction per Government Code 11350 to hear this case. The complaint does not seek to enjoin or prevent the collection of a tax. The plaintiff is not required to exhaust an administrative remedy. The purported regulation is sufficiently alleged and the complaint is not uncertain. Judge Kahn discloses that he used to work with both plaintiffs' counsel at the same law firm (Heller Ehrman White & McAuliffe) more than twenty years ago and that, after he left that law firm, he periodically remained in contact with Mr. Leon until approximately 2002 including referring cases to Mr. Leon and that, when he was appointed to the bench in 2001, he arranged for several of his cases to be transferred to Mr. Leon. Judge Kahn believes that he is and can remain fair and impartial to both sides. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
